title: Settings UI with Config Validation & Save-Reload Pattern
summary: Pattern for building a local settings UI for desktop apps: TOML config file reading and writing with unknown field preservation, in-process validation before save, atomic file writes to prevent corruption, and hot reload notification so running services pick up changes without restart. The UI layer is separated from the config service layer, allowing the same config backend to serve both a GUI editor and CLI/API consumers.
why_reusable: Every desktop tool, agent, or assistant eventually needs a settings panel. The pattern of TOML schema + validation + atomic save + hot reload generalizes to any config-driven desktop or server application. The separation of UI from config service means the same backend can power a settings window, a CLI `config set` command, or an API endpoint.
adapter_target: Generic settings config service — adapter extracts the config read/validate/write/reload logic into a reusable `ConfigService` class that any UI or CLI can consume.
implementation_notes: Use TOML for human-editable config; JSON schema for validation
Atomic write: write to .tmp file, validate, then rename over original
Preserve unknown/comment fields on read→modify→write cycle
Hot reload: watch file mtime or use a reload signal (e.g. internal event bus)
Separate config schema definition from UI layout definition
Validation errors should return field-level messages, not raw exceptions
tests_required: Unit test: config read with missing optional fields (defaults applied)
Unit test: config write preserves unknown fields
Unit test: atomic write does not corrupt on mid-write crash
Unit test: validation rejects invalid values with field-level errors
Integration test: save via UI → verify file content → trigger reload → verify service picks up change
risks: TOML has subtle type coercion rules; validate types explicitly after parse
Atomic rename may fail across filesystem boundaries; test on target OS
Hot reload can race with in-flight operations using old config; version the config or use a generation counter
Unknown field preservation adds complexity — decide whether it's worth it for the use case
do_not_do: Do not write config directly from UI without validation pass
Do not overwrite the config file in-place (use atomic tmp+rename)
Do not couple UI widget layout to config schema structure
Do not reload config mid-operation; defer to operation boundary
packaging_impact: Minimal — TOML parsing is stdlib in Python 3.11+. jsonschema adds ~200KB if used.
notes: Derived from xiaohuang settings_ui.py and settings_config_file_service.py patterns. The reusable artifact is the config service pattern, not the specific UI layout.
